roadmap.notes: Lands ADR-0065 (amends ADR-0064 §2/§3/§5/§6): workflow body returns Result<Output, TerminalError>; the status enum becomes the engine-owned WorkflowStatus control-plane projection; typed WorkflowStart.input crosses Raft with rkyv-envelope discipline (resolves #217); retry budget lives in the engine/journal (D4). Greenfield SINGLE CUT — WorkflowResult is DELETED as a body return; no old/new coexistence as a shipped end-state. THE GOVERNING CONSTRAINT is the lefthook pre-commit (cargo xtask lima run -- cargo clippy + nextest-affected): every step's COMMIT must leave the workspace compiling and every test green (--no-verify is forbidden). 'Single cut' is the END STATE, not a license for a non-compiling intermediate commit. The decomposition is therefore additive-first (Phase 01 steps 01-01/01-02 introduce types that coexist with WorkflowResult, each green on its own new unit tests) followed by ONE necessarily-large atomic step (01-03) that reshapes the Workflow trait, creates the erased trait/adapter, drives the engine, deletes WorkflowResult, and migrates the entire ~34-file blast radius (146 WorkflowResult occurrences) in a single green commit — because reshaping run()'s signature breaks the keystone fixture (crates/overdrive-core/src/testing/workflow.rs), every Workflow impl, the engine, the journal, the obs row, the lifecycle reconciler, and the sim replay-equivalence evaluator SIMULTANEOUSLY. That cut cannot be split into independently-green sub-steps. Phase 01 (Slice 01) + the engine-drive half of Slice 02 collapse into 01-03 (compiler-forced co-landing); Phase 02 carries only Slice 02's genuinely-additive new DST invariant. Phase 03 (Slice 03) discharges #217 (started_digests off spec.input; persist/hydrate full spec; remove the TODO(#217) marker). Phase 04 (Slice 04 — IN SCOPE per dispatch) is additive on the stable Slice-01 body contract: the retry-re-drive loop, RetryAttempted journal command, engine-constant budget policy, and engine-minted BudgetExhausted. NOTE ON THE SCHEMA-EVOLUTION FIXTURE: the dispatch's slice→step mapping placed fixture wiring (uncomment mod workflow_start; + mint FIXTURE_V1) in Slice 03, but this roadmap wires it in 01-02 — the same step that CREATES WorkflowStartEnvelope — because ADR-0048 §1 mandates every rkyv envelope ship its per-version golden fixture in the same commit that introduces the type, and deferring the fixture two steps would leave the envelope un-pinned (a review rejection). This is the green-correct AND ADR-0048-correct placement; see 01-02 implementation_notes. The five DISTILL scaffolds activate as: NEW-1 (workflow_typed_output_roundtrip) in 01-03; NEW-3 (schema_evolution/workflow_start) in 01-02; NEW-2 (workflow_input_digest_divergence) + NEW-4 (workflow_malformed_input_terminal) in 03-01; NEW-5 (workflow_budget_exhaustion_mints_terminal) in 04-01. No new dependencies; no proprietary tech. Test paradigm per .claude/rules/testing.md (Rust #[test]/#[tokio::test], no .feature files); RED scaffolds already shipped by DISTILL as #[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")].

phases.name: Core types + the typed/erased trait split (additive types first, then the atomic single-cut)
phases.description: Slice 01. Introduce the new value types that can COEXIST with the old WorkflowResult contract (TerminalError, WorkflowStatus — step 01-01; WorkflowStart.input + WorkflowStartEnvelope V1 + typed codec + golden fixture — step 01-02), each green on its own new unit tests while the engine still drives WorkflowResult. Then 01-03 performs the necessarily-large atomic cut: reshape the Workflow trait to run(ctx, input) -> Result<Output, TerminalError> with type Input/Output, create ErasedWorkflow + ErasedWorkflowAdapter<W>, drive the engine via run_erased, map body return → WorkflowStatus, swap the journal Terminal / obs row / lifecycle terminal carried types, DELETE WorkflowResult, and migrate the entire breaking blast radius (keystone fixture + all impls + ~24 MIGRATE tests + the sim evaluator's 23 WorkflowResult sites) in one green commit. 01-03 absorbs Slice 02's engine-drive work because the compiler will not accept a half-reshaped trait — they are one commit.

steps.name: Create TerminalError + TerminalErrorKind and WorkflowStatus (additive core types; coexist with WorkflowResult); trybuild non-substitutability against TerminalCondition
steps.description: Additive-first step: introduce the two new core value types ALONGSIDE the still-present WorkflowResult and the still-unchanged Workflow trait. TerminalError { kind: TerminalErrorKind, detail: String } with kind ∈ {Explicit, BudgetExhausted, MalformedInput, OutputEncode} (#[non_exhaustive] on TerminalErrorKind), validating length-capping constructors (TerminalError::explicit / ::malformed_input / ::output_encode), and a pub(crate)-from-the-engine budget_exhausted() the author cannot reach (the engine mints it in Slice 04). serde Serialize/Deserialize + Debug/Clone/PartialEq/Eq. WorkflowStatus { Completed { output: Vec<u8> } | Failed { terminal: TerminalError } | Cancelled | TimedOut } (#[non_exhaustive]; Cancelled/TimedOut are forward variants the Phase-1 engine never writes), serde + the same derives. Both live in overdrive-core::workflow and are re-exported from lib.rs. NOTHING consumes them yet — the engine, journal, obs row, and lifecycle reconciler still carry WorkflowResult; the trait is untouched. This keeps the commit green: new types + new unit tests, zero migration. Per ADR-0065 § Consequences (Negative) three terminal-modelling types must never be conflated (TerminalError, WorkflowStatus, TerminalCondition) — add the feature-delta-RECOMMENDED trybuild compile-fail case proving a fn(WorkflowStatus) rejects a TerminalCondition and vice versa (the .stderr fixture is generated against the real compiler now that both types exist).
steps.criteria: TerminalError + TerminalErrorKind + WorkflowStatus exist in overdrive-core::workflow with the exact variant sets from ADR-0065 §2/§3, #[non_exhaustive] where specified, validating length-capping detail constructors, serde + Debug/Clone/PartialEq/Eq derives; both re-exported from overdrive-core::lib; WorkflowResult and the Workflow trait are UNCHANGED (still present, still used) so the commit is green with no migration; serde round-trip property tests cover every TerminalErrorKind variant and every WorkflowStatus variant; the trybuild compile-fail case proves WorkflowStatus and TerminalCondition are non-substitutable.
acceptance_criteria: TerminalError is a struct { kind: TerminalErrorKind, detail: String } with serde::Serialize + serde::Deserialize + Debug + Clone + PartialEq + Eq; TerminalErrorKind is #[non_exhaustive] enum { Explicit, BudgetExhausted, MalformedInput, OutputEncode }

TerminalError exposes validating constructors TerminalError::explicit(detail), ::malformed_input(detail), ::output_encode(detail) that length-cap detail at construction (per ADR-0065 §2 — closes the free-text replay-determinism hazard); a detail exceeding the cap is truncated deterministically (a property test asserts the capped length is stable for over-long input)

TerminalError::budget_exhausted() is reachable ONLY from the engine vantage (pub(crate) or equivalent) — author/test code in overdrive-core cannot construct BudgetExhausted via a public constructor; verifiable by `rg 'pub fn budget_exhausted' crates/overdrive-core/src` returning zero matches (only a pub(crate) form exists)

WorkflowStatus is #[non_exhaustive] enum { Completed { output: Vec<u8> }, Failed { terminal: TerminalError }, Cancelled, TimedOut } with serde + Debug + Clone + PartialEq + Eq; TerminalError exposes a kind() accessor returning TerminalErrorKind
Both types are re-exported from crates/overdrive-core/src/lib.rs (the obs row and journal will carry them in 01-03)

WorkflowResult still exists and the Workflow trait signature is unchanged at this step — `rg 'enum WorkflowResult' crates/overdrive-core/src/workflow/mod.rs` still matches; the commit compiles and all existing workflow tests stay green with ZERO migration in this step

proptest TerminalErrorRoundtrip and WorkflowStatusRoundtrip — for every Arbitrary value (covering all four TerminalErrorKind variants and all four WorkflowStatus variants), encode → decode → equal-to-original under serde

trybuild compile-fail fixture workflow_status_vs_terminal_condition.rs proves a function typed fn(WorkflowStatus) rejects a TerminalCondition argument and a fn(TerminalCondition) rejects a WorkflowStatus (per ADR-0065 § Consequences Negative; the .stderr is checked in and pinned)
cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-core --features integration-tests passes on Linux; cargo nextest run -p overdrive-core --features integration-tests --no-run on macOS
cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-core --file crates/overdrive-core/src/workflow/mod.rs kill rate >= 80%
steps.implementation_notes: ADDITIVE-FIRST — the whole point of this step is to land green WITHOUT touching the Workflow trait or WorkflowResult. The two new types have no consumers yet; the engine/journal/obs/lifecycle migration to WorkflowStatus is 01-03's job. Do NOT begin the trait reshape here. TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): unit tests for the two new types MUST be property-based (proptest @given-style strategies over the variant space) per .claude/rules/testing.md § 'Property-based testing' (newtype-completeness / serde roundtrip is a mandatory PBT call site); single-example tests are FALLBACK only (document with a # bypass: comment if used). The trybuild case is EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: its load-bearing artifact is the .stderr fixture (exact compiler diagnostic), not a property — compensate with the serde-roundtrip property tests on the adjacent unit surface. Wire the new acceptance file into overdrive-core/tests/acceptance.rs's mod block; wire the compile-fail fixture per the existing tests/compile_fail/ convention (intent_vs_observation.rs precedent). The detail length-cap closes ADR-0064 §3's reason: String hazard — pin the cap as a named const and assert truncation determinism.

steps.name: Extend WorkflowStart with input: Vec<u8>; add WorkflowStartEnvelope (rkyv V1) + co-located typed codec; wire + mint the schema-evolution golden fixture (NEW-3)
steps.description: Additive-first step (the WorkflowStart.input surface + its durable-intent codec land while the engine still hashes spec.name and still drives WorkflowResult — the engine's #217 discharge is Slice 03). Grow WorkflowStart from { name: WorkflowName } to { name: WorkflowName, input: Vec<u8> } (the opaque CBOR W::Input; an INPUT per development.md § 'Persist inputs, not derived state'). Create WorkflowStartEnvelope (V1(WorkflowStartV1)) + the VersionedEnvelope impl + the co-located typed codec WorkflowStart::archive_for_store / WorkflowStart::from_store_bytes on the typed value (the ADR-0048 §4b Job precedent: rkyv versioned envelope wrapping the OUTER WorkflowStart only — the inner input stays opaque CBOR; the byte-level IntentStore surface is unchanged). Adding the non-defaulted input field breaks every WorkflowStart { name } construction site (test fixtures + action-shim); fix them mechanically by adding input (e.g. an empty or unit-CBOR vec) in the SAME commit — a green compile-fixup, NOT the trait cut. Then WIRE the already-authored NEW-3 schema-evolution fixture: run print_fixture_v1_bytes to mint FIXTURE_V1, pin GOLDEN_DISCRIMINANT_OFFSET_V1, and UNCOMMENT mod workflow_start; in crates/overdrive-core/tests/schema_evolution.rs (line 53). Per ADR-0048 §1 the golden fixture MUST ship in the same commit that introduces the envelope type.
steps.criteria: WorkflowStart carries { name: WorkflowName, input: Vec<u8> }; WorkflowStartEnvelope (V1) + VersionedEnvelope impl + WorkflowStart::archive_for_store / from_store_bytes exist co-located on the typed value per ADR-0048 §4b; every prior WorkflowStart { name } construction site is migrated to include input in the same commit (green compile); the NEW-3 fixture is wired (mod uncommented, FIXTURE_V1 minted, discriminant offset pinned) and its three #[test]s pass; WorkflowResult and the Workflow trait remain unchanged at this step.
acceptance_criteria: WorkflowStart is { name: WorkflowName, input: Vec<u8> } where input is the opaque CBOR-encoded W::Input (documented as an input per development.md § 'Persist inputs, not derived state'; the engine does not decode it)

WorkflowStartEnvelope is a per-type rkyv enum with exactly V1(WorkflowStartV1) today; it derives rkyv::{Archive, Serialize, Deserialize}; it implements VersionedEnvelope with type Latest = WorkflowStartV1, latest(), and into_latest() per ADR-0048; the public alias WorkflowStart points at the V1 payload (alias-to-payload, UI-02) so call sites use struct-literal WorkflowStart { name, input }

WorkflowStart::archive_for_store(&self) -> Result<Vec<u8>, _> wraps via WorkflowStartEnvelope::latest and rkyv-serialises; WorkflowStart::from_store_bytes(bytes) rkyv-deserialises into the envelope and projects via into_latest(); a malformed/unknown-version byte sequence surfaces EnvelopeError (the intent fail-fast precursor, ADR-0065 §5)

The rkyv envelope wraps the OUTER WorkflowStart only; the inner input: Vec<u8> stays opaque CBOR (the 'aggregate envelopes wrap the outer type only' rule; rkyv outer / CBOR inner stay separate per development.md § 'rkyv schema evolution')

Every prior WorkflowStart { name } construction site (action-shim persist_workflow_intents, test fixtures, e2e trigger reconcilers) is updated to construct { name, input } in THIS commit so the workspace compiles green; verifiable: `rg 'WorkflowStart \{ name' crates/` shows no remaining name-only literal

NEW-3 fixture wired: FIXTURE_V1 in crates/overdrive-core/tests/schema_evolution/workflow_start.rs is the real hex (no __RED_SCAFFOLD__ placeholder remains), GOLDEN_DISCRIMINANT_OFFSET_V1 is the real pinned value (not 0-placeholder), and `mod workflow_start;` is UNCOMMENTED in crates/overdrive-core/tests/schema_evolution.rs (line 53)

The three NEW-3 #[test]s pass: workflow_start_envelope_v1_golden_bytes_roundtrip, workflow_start_envelope_discriminant_offset_triangulates, workflow_start_envelope_unknown_version_probe_surfaces_error; the #[ignore]d print_fixture_v1_bytes regenerator remains present
`rg 'enum WorkflowResult' crates/overdrive-core/src/workflow/mod.rs` still matches and the Workflow trait signature is unchanged — this step does NOT begin the trait cut
cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-core --features integration-tests passes on Linux (incl. --test schema_evolution); --no-run on macOS

cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-core --file crates/overdrive-core/src/workflow/mod.rs kill rate >= 80% (covers archive_for_store / from_store_bytes / into_latest branches)
steps.implementation_notes: DEPARTURE FROM THE DISPATCH SLICE MAP (deliberate, green-correct): the dispatch placed schema-evolution fixture wiring in Slice 03. This roadmap wires it HERE because ADR-0048 §1 mandates every rkyv envelope ship its per-version golden fixture in the SAME commit that introduces the envelope type — deferring two steps would leave WorkflowStartEnvelope un-pinned across 01-02 and 01-03 (a review rejection per development.md § 'rkyv schema evolution'). Slice 03 (03-01) therefore owns ONLY the engine-side #217 discharge (started_digests + persist/hydrate using the codec created here), not the fixture. The action-shim edit in THIS step is the minimal compile-fixup to add input to its WorkflowStart construction (it still persists spec.name bytes for now — the full archive_for_store persist + from_store_bytes hydrate is 03-01; here we only keep the workspace compiling after the field addition). ADDITIVE-FIRST: do NOT touch the Workflow trait or WorkflowResult. TEST PARADIGM: the schema-evolution golden-bytes test is EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM — its load-bearing artifact is the pinned FIXTURE_V1 hex (a golden-file diff), not a property; compensate with a proptest at the unit level on the WorkflowStart serde/codec roundtrip (archive_for_store → from_store_bytes → equal) on the adjacent slot. Pre-shipment FIXTURE_V1 regeneration is allowed (feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations.md); after V1 ships it is immutable.

steps.name: ATOMIC SINGLE-CUT — reshape Workflow trait + ErasedWorkflow/Adapter + engine run_erased + body-return→WorkflowStatus mapping; DELETE WorkflowResult; migrate the entire blast radius (keystone fixture + all impls + ~24 MIGRATE tests + the sim evaluator); activate NEW-1
steps.description: THE NECESSARILY-LARGE STEP. Reshaping Workflow::run to run(&self, ctx, input: Self::Input) -> Result<Self::Output, TerminalError> with type Input/Output breaks the keystone fixture (crates/overdrive-core/src/testing/workflow.rs — the three ProvisionRecord* workflows), every Workflow impl, the engine, the journal Terminal command, the ObservationRow::WorkflowTerminal variant, the lifecycle reconciler's WorkflowInstanceState.terminal, AND the sim replay-equivalence evaluator — ALL AT ONCE. There is no green intermediate; this is one commit. It MUST: (1) reshape the Workflow trait (type Output, type Input, run(ctx, input) -> Result<Output, TerminalError>); (2) create ErasedWorkflow { run_erased(&self, ctx, input_bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<Vec<u8>, TerminalError> } + the generic ErasedWorkflowAdapter<W> blanket impl (decode input_bytes → W::Input via ciborium, mapping decode failure to TerminalError::malformed_input; call the typed body; encode W::Output → CBOR, mapping encode failure to TerminalError::output_encode); (3) reshape WorkflowRegistry/WorkflowFactory to map WorkflowName → factory<Box<dyn ErasedWorkflow>>; (4) migrate WorkflowEngine::start to drive run_erased(&ctx, &input_bytes) and map Ok(bytes) → WorkflowStatus::Completed { output: bytes }, Err(TerminalError) → WorkflowStatus::Failed { terminal }, and the catch_unwind panic path → Failed { TerminalError::explicit(<deterministic downcast detail>) } (ADR-0064 path retargeted, byte-stable); (5) swap JournalCommand::Terminal { result: WorkflowResult } → { status: WorkflowStatus }, ObservationRow::WorkflowTerminal { result } → { status }, WorkflowInstanceState.terminal → Option<WorkflowStatus>, and workflow_terminal_rows() return type → Vec<(CorrelationKey, WorkflowStatus)>; (6) DELETE WorkflowResult; (7) migrate the keystone fixture (each WorkflowResult::Success → Ok(()), each Failed { reason } → Err(TerminalError::explicit(detail)), Output=()/Input=(), add spec.input via ciborium of ()) and every other Workflow impl across crates; (8) migrate the ~24 MIGRATE tests per the DISTILL canonical assertion mapping (core M-c1..M-c3 incl. the two syn-scan reference-body consts; cp M1-M8; e2e M9-M11; sim M12-M19; the M-inv test surface); (9) migrate the sim evaluator's 23 WorkflowResult sites (replay_equivalence_provision_record terminal assertion → Completed { output } round-tripping to ()) + the sim journal proptest generator (workflow_result_strategy → a WorkflowStatus strategy) + sim/store-local observation_store; (10) ACTIVATE NEW-1 (workflow_typed_output_roundtrip — replace both #[should_panic] bodies with the real ErasedWorkflowAdapter roundtrip, promoting the output test to proptest); (11) introduce the WorkflowTerminalStatusProjection DST invariant VARIANT as a todo!("RED scaffold: ...") evaluator body kept OUT of the active cargo dst dispatch loop, so the exhaustive match compiles and the gate stays green — 02-01 fills it. Per ADR-0048 intent asymmetry, do NOT discharge the #217 started_digests/persist here (that is 03-01); started_digests may continue hashing spec.name at this step (still green), or be moved in 03-01.
steps.criteria: Workflow trait is { type Output; type Input; async fn run(&self, ctx, input: Self::Input) -> Result<Self::Output, TerminalError> }; ErasedWorkflow + ErasedWorkflowAdapter<W> exist and the engine holds Box<dyn ErasedWorkflow> driven via run_erased; the body return → WorkflowStatus mapping is exactly ADR-0065 §3; WorkflowResult is DELETED everywhere (rg zero matches across crates/); journal Terminal / obs row / lifecycle terminal / workflow_terminal_rows all carry WorkflowStatus; the keystone fixture + every Workflow impl + all ~24 MIGRATE tests + the sim evaluator's 23 sites are migrated; NEW-1 is active and green; the workspace compiles and every test passes at this single commit; the WorkflowTerminalStatusProjection variant exists as a green todo! scaffold not yet harness-dispatched.
acceptance_criteria: Workflow trait in overdrive-core::workflow is exactly { type Output: Serialize+DeserializeOwned+Send+Sync; type Input: Serialize+DeserializeOwned+Send+Sync; async fn run(&self, ctx: &WorkflowCtx, input: Self::Input) -> Result<Self::Output, TerminalError> } (per ADR-0065 §1); verified by grep on the trait definition

ErasedWorkflow { async fn run_erased(&self, ctx: &WorkflowCtx, input_bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<Vec<u8>, TerminalError> } is object-safe (Box<dyn ErasedWorkflow> compiles — a trybuild compile-pass or a live Box<dyn ErasedWorkflow> binding proves it); ErasedWorkflowAdapter<W: Workflow> blanket-impls it: ciborium-decode input_bytes → W::Input (decode err → TerminalError::malformed_input), call self.0.run, ciborium-encode W::Output (encode err → TerminalError::output_encode)

WorkflowRegistry/WorkflowFactory map WorkflowName → Box<dyn Fn() -> Box<dyn ErasedWorkflow>>; the composition root registers a typed workflow and the adapter is applied internally (the author never writes the erasure)

WorkflowEngine::start resolves the factory, gets Box<dyn ErasedWorkflow>, calls run_erased(&ctx, &spec.input), and maps: Ok(bytes) → WorkflowStatus::Completed { output: bytes }; Err(TerminalError) → WorkflowStatus::Failed { terminal }; author-body panic (catch_unwind) → WorkflowStatus::Failed { terminal: TerminalError::explicit(<deterministic downcast detail — the &str/String panic message, never the address-bearing box>) } (ADR-0064 path carried forward and retargeted, byte-stable across runs)

JournalCommand::Terminal carries status: WorkflowStatus (was result: WorkflowResult); ObservationRow::WorkflowTerminal carries status: WorkflowStatus; WorkflowInstanceState.terminal is Option<WorkflowStatus>; workflow_terminal_rows() returns Vec<(CorrelationKey, WorkflowStatus)> — every caller updated

WorkflowResult is DELETED: `rg 'WorkflowResult' crates/ -l` returns ZERO matches (production AND tests; the enum, all construction sites, all match sites, the sim proptest generator, all rustdoc references) — per § Deletion discipline the type and everything naming it die in this commit

The keystone fixture crates/overdrive-core/src/testing/workflow.rs (ProvisionRecord / *WithSleep / *WithSignalEmit) is migrated to type Output=(), type Input=(), run(&self, ctx, _input: ()) -> Result<(), TerminalError> with each old Success → Ok(()) and each old Failed { reason } → Err(TerminalError::explicit(detail)), and each spec() helper sets input via ciborium::into_writer(&(), ..)

All ~24 MIGRATE tests are migrated per the DISTILL canonical assertion mapping: core M-c1 workflow_trait_drives_to_terminal, M-c2 workflow_body_has_no_step_machine (syn-scan reference-body const updated to the new signature), M-c3 workflow_body_routes_nondeterminism_through_ctx (same); cp M2/M5/M6/M7/M8 + the M3 panic test (matches!(status, WorkflowStatus::Failed { terminal } if terminal.kind() == TerminalErrorKind::Explicit)); the M4 compile-fixup; e2e M9/M10; sim M12-M18 (terminal helper return + obs-row rename + Some(Completed)) — M1/M11/M19's input_digest assertion target is deferred to 03-01 if started_digests is not moved here, otherwise migrated here

The sim replay-equivalence evaluator (crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/evaluators.rs, the 23 WorkflowResult occurrences) is migrated: replay_equivalence_provision_record's terminal assertion changes from 'terminal is Success' to 'the WorkflowStatus projection is Completed { output } and the erased output round-trips to ()'; the byte-identical-trajectory + bounded-progress core is unchanged

NEW-1 is ACTIVE and green: crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/workflow_typed_output_roundtrip.rs's two functions no longer #[should_panic] — erased_workflow_round_trips_a_non_unit_typed_output_through_cbor drives ErasedWorkflowAdapter over a non-unit Output and asserts ciborium-decode equals the body's value (promoted to proptest over a domain Output strategy); erased_workflow_decodes_typed_input_from_recorded_cbor_bytes pins the Input decode side

The WorkflowTerminalStatusProjection invariant variant EXISTS in overdrive-sim::invariants::Invariant with an evaluate() body of todo!("RED scaffold: WorkflowTerminalStatusProjection lands GREEN in step 02-01") gated #[expect(clippy::todo, reason = ...)], keeping the exhaustive match intact; it is NOT yet wired into the active cargo dst scenario-evaluation loop, so cargo dst stays GREEN; existing invariants (replay_equivalence_provision_record, WorkflowExactlyOnceEffectOnResume) continue to fire

cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests passes (every crate, every migrated test); cargo xtask lima run -- cargo dst passes; --no-run on macOS as the pre-ship gate

cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-core --file crates/overdrive-core/src/workflow/mod.rs AND --package overdrive-control-plane --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/workflow_runtime/mod.rs --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/journal/mod.rs each achieve kill rate >= 80%
steps.implementation_notes: THIS IS THE ONE HONEST LARGE STEP — it cannot be split into independently-green sub-steps and the roadmap does not pretend otherwise. The compiler is the forcing function: WorkflowResult appears in 34 files (146 occurrences); deleting it + reshaping run() breaks the keystone fixture, every impl, the engine, the journal, the obs row, the lifecycle reconciler, and the sim evaluator SIMULTANEOUSLY, so they migrate in ONE commit. DES still runs this as a single RED→GREEN→COMMIT cycle (the RED scaffolds NEW-1 + the breaking compile errors ARE the RED; GREEN is the full migration). What is NOT in this step (deliberately deferred to keep it bounded and to respect the slice map): (a) the #217 started_digests discharge + persist-full-spec/hydrate-from-store-bytes loop → 03-01 (started_digests may keep hashing spec.name here, still green; or move it — both are green, but 03-01 owns the #217 ACs and NEW-2); (b) the WorkflowTerminalStatusProjection invariant BODY → 02-01 (here it is a todo! scaffold kept out of the active dst dispatch so cargo dst stays green, per .claude/rules/testing.md § 'Production-side scaffolds' — keep the exhaustive match intact, do NOT short-circuit the harness with a neutral stub); (c) NEW-2/NEW-4 → 03-01, NEW-5 → 04-01 (they stay #[should_panic] RED here). Migration recipes per scaffold are in each scaffold's rustdoc + the DISTILL canonical assertion mapping table (feature-delta § 'Reuse / Migrate / New triage'). The 3 REUSE tests (workflow_ctx_run_and_sleep, workflow_committed_step_survives_crash, workflow_journal_write_ordering) and workflow_sleep_records_deadline_not_remaining have UNCHANGED source — they are not edited; their test binaries just need to compile around the migrated siblings. TEST PARADIGM: NEW-1's output roundtrip is a universal property — promote it to proptest over a domain Output strategy (Mandate 9, Stage-0 paradigm-from-day-zero); the migrated tests retain their existing example/property shape (they verify wiring + the body-return→status mapping, not new invariants). Per development.md § 'Production code is not shaped by simulation' — do NOT add engine logic whose only purpose is to make a Sim adapter behave; the run_erased/ status mapping is the genuine production shape. Do NOT discharge #217 here.

phases.name: Slice 02 additive — fill the WorkflowTerminalStatusProjection DST invariant GREEN
phases.description: Slice 02's genuinely-additive deliverable. The engine-drive + body-return→WorkflowStatus mapping + the full test/evaluator migration already co-landed in 01-03 (compiler-forced). This phase fills the WorkflowTerminalStatusProjection DST invariant — introduced as a green todo! scaffold variant in 01-03 — with its real evaluator body and wires it into the active cargo dst dispatch. It is genuinely green-splittable from the keystone because every type it needs already exists; the variant is present (returning todo!) so the exhaustive match already compiles, and this step only fills the body + wires the dispatch + lands the acceptance assertion.

steps.name: Fill the WorkflowTerminalStatusProjection DST invariant GREEN; wire into cargo dst; pin the body-return→status mapping as a structural property
steps.description: Replace the 01-03 todo!("RED scaffold") body of the WorkflowTerminalStatusProjection invariant with the real evaluator and wire it into the active cargo dst scenario-evaluation loop. The evaluator drives a workflow returning Err(TerminalError::explicit(...)) and asserts the engine writes WorkflowStatus::Failed { terminal } (NOT a contentless variant) with the TerminalError round-tripping byte-equal through the journal Terminal command AND the observation row — pinning the body-return → status-projection mapping (D3) as a structural DST property (ADR-0065 § 'DST invariants', NEW invariant). Per the house no-inline-literal convention the invariant is a named enum variant with its evaluator in evaluators.rs. Additive on the stable Slice-01 body contract — green.
steps.criteria: WorkflowTerminalStatusProjection's evaluator body is implemented (no todo! remains); the invariant is dispatched by cargo dst on every run; it drives an Err(TerminalError::explicit) workflow and asserts WorkflowStatus::Failed { terminal } round-trips byte-equal through the journal Terminal command + the obs row; existing invariants continue to fire; the DST seed prints on failure and reproduces bit-identically.
acceptance_criteria: The WorkflowTerminalStatusProjection variant's evaluate() body no longer contains todo!/panic — `rg 'todo!.*WorkflowTerminalStatusProjection' crates/overdrive-sim/src` returns zero matches; the #[expect(clippy::todo)] scaffold gate is removed (it self-removes when the lint stops firing)

The evaluator drives a workflow whose body returns Err(TerminalError::explicit(detail)) and asserts the engine writes WorkflowStatus::Failed { terminal } (NOT Completed, NOT a contentless variant) where terminal.kind() == TerminalErrorKind::Explicit and terminal.detail() == detail

The invariant asserts the SAME TerminalError round-trips byte-equal through BOTH the journal JournalCommand::Terminal { status } AND the ObservationRow::WorkflowTerminal { status } (the durable terminal and the observable terminal carry identical bytes — pins D3's lossless projection)
WorkflowTerminalStatusProjection is wired into the cargo dst dispatch (the harness evaluates it against its scenario); failure of this invariant fails the gate
Existing invariants (replay_equivalence_provision_record, WorkflowExactlyOnceEffectOnResume, and the rest of the catalogue) continue to fire — the new variant is additive, not a replacement
The invariant prints its DST seed on failure; reproduction with cargo dst --seed <N> is bit-identical (per .claude/rules/testing.md § 'Seeding and reproducibility')
cargo xtask lima run -- cargo dst passes with the new invariant active; cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-sim --features integration-tests passes
cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-sim --file crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/evaluators.rs kill rate >= 80%
steps.implementation_notes: This is the GREEN fill of the production-side RED scaffold introduced in 01-03 (the todo! variant). Per .claude/rules/testing.md § 'Production-side scaffolds' the GREEN transition is removing the todo! body + the #[expect(clippy::todo)] gate and landing the real evaluator. DST invariants are NOT #[should_panic] test scaffolds — they are the named variant + evaluator, exercised by cargo dst (so this step's test_file is the evaluator itself; the example roadmap's 01-07 sets the same precedent of pointing test_file at evaluators.rs). TEST PARADIGM: a DST invariant is an assert_always!/assert_eventually!-style structural property over the harness trajectory (the paradigm's native shape), not a single-example unit test — no exemption needed. Per ADR-0065 § 'DST invariants' this pins the body-return → status-projection mapping (D3) as a property the byte-identical-trajectory harness enforces forever (complementing NEW-1's adapter-level roundtrip and the migrated tests' wiring checks).

phases.name: Slice 03 — typed WorkflowStart crosses Raft; #217 discharged (started_digests off spec.input; persist/hydrate full spec)
phases.description: Slice 03. Discharge #217 at the engine + composition boundary now that WorkflowStart.input + the rkyv codec exist (01-02) and the trait/engine are reshaped (01-03). The engine's started_digests derives input_digest = ContentHash::of(&spec.input) and the TODO(#217) marker (workflow_runtime/mod.rs lines 549-556) is REMOVED; the action-shim persists the full spec via spec.archive_for_store()? and the lifecycle reconciler's hydrate_desired reads WorkflowStart::from_store_bytes(value)? (replacing the WorkflowName::new(from_utf8(..)) parse), with a malformed/undecodable intent refusing per the ADR-0048 intent asymmetry. Migrate the 3 input_digest-asserting tests (M1, M11, M19) from ContentHash::of(ProvisionRecord::PAYLOAD) to ContentHash::of(&spec.input). Activate NEW-2 (input_digest divergence — the executable #217 acceptance) and NEW-4 (malformed input ⇒ engine-minted Failed{MalformedInput}, body never entered). Additive/refining on the stable body contract — green.

steps.name: started_digests off spec.input (remove TODO(#217)); action-shim persists full spec via archive_for_store; lifecycle hydrate_desired via from_store_bytes; migrate the 3 input_digest tests; activate NEW-2 + NEW-4; close #217
steps.description: The #217 value-side + read-side fix. (1) WorkflowEngine::started_digests derives input_digest = ContentHash::of(&spec.input) (start-input bytes) and spec_digest = ContentHash::of(spec.name…) (kind identity) — the two diverge as intended; DELETE the TODO(#217) comment block at workflow_runtime/mod.rs lines 549-556. (2) action_shim::persist_workflow_intents persists the FULL spec via spec.archive_for_store()? under IntentKey::for_workflow_instance(correlation) — NOT spec.name.as_str().as_bytes() (the #217 bug); the codec was created in 01-02. (3) the lifecycle reconciler's hydrate_desired (reconciler_runtime.rs) reads WorkflowStart::from_store_bytes(value)? — replacing the WorkflowName::new(from_utf8(value)) parse; a decode failure on intent is load-bearing (intent SSOT) and refuses with a structured health.startup.refused-class surface (ADR-0048 intent asymmetry). (4) Migrate M1 (workflow_engine_writes_terminal_row), M11 (journal_writes_to_redb), M19 (journal_records_inputs_not_derived) input_digest assertions to ContentHash::of(&spec.input) (CBOR of () for the unit fixture — NOT the step payload b"provision-record"). (5) ACTIVATE NEW-2 (two distinct inputs of one kind ⇒ distinct input_digests; same input ⇒ stable digest + persist→rehydrate fidelity) and NEW-4 (undecodable input ⇒ engine-minted Failed{MalformedInput}, body never run, clean teardown) — replace their #[should_panic] bodies with the real engine-driven assertions.
steps.criteria: started_digests derives input_digest from spec.input and the TODO(#217) marker is gone; the action-shim persists the full spec via archive_for_store and the lifecycle reconciler hydrates via from_store_bytes with malformed-intent refusal; the 3 input_digest tests assert against ContentHash::of(&spec.input); NEW-2 and NEW-4 are active and green; #217 is closed (the executable divergence acceptance passes).
acceptance_criteria: WorkflowEngine::started_digests derives input_digest = ContentHash::of(&spec.input) and spec_digest = ContentHash::of over the kind identity (spec.name); the two diverge for one kind with different inputs and converge for the same input — verified by NEW-2

The TODO(#217) comment block in crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/workflow_runtime/mod.rs (the lines that today read `ContentHash::of(spec.name.as_str().as_bytes())` for input_digest) is REMOVED: `rg 'TODO\(#217\)' crates/` returns zero matches AND input_digest is no longer derived from spec.name

action_shim::persist_workflow_intents persists spec.archive_for_store()? bytes (the full name+input spec) under the instance intent key — NOT spec.name.as_str().as_bytes(); `rg 'spec.name.as_str\(\).as_bytes\(\)' crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/action_shim` returns zero matches

The lifecycle reconciler's hydrate_desired (crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs) reads WorkflowStart::from_store_bytes(value)? instead of WorkflowName::new(from_utf8(value)); a malformed/undecodable intent surfaces a structured refusal (health.startup.refused-class / typed error per ADR-0048 intent asymmetry), not a silent skip

M1 workflow_engine_writes_terminal_row, M11 journal_writes_to_redb, M19 journal_records_inputs_not_derived assert input_digest == ContentHash::of(&spec.input) (CBOR of () for the unit ProvisionRecord fixture) — NOT ContentHash::of(ProvisionRecord::PAYLOAD); the b"provision-record" step-payload assertion is gone

NEW-2 active and green: two_distinct_inputs_of_one_kind_get_distinct_input_digests (assert_ne on input_digests for distinct inputs, assert_eq on spec_digests for the same kind) and same_input_round_trips_with_a_stable_input_digest (persist via archive_for_store → hydrate via from_store_bytes → input byte-equal → identical input_digest) — both #[should_panic] removed

NEW-4 active and green: undecodable_input_terminates_failed_malformed_input_without_running_the_body — a spec.input that fails ciborium::from_reader::<W::Input> drives the engine to write WorkflowStatus::Failed { terminal } with terminal.kind() == MalformedInput, the live-instance entry is torn down (no strand), and the body's run-counter is 0 (the decode gate fired before the body)
cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests passes (incl. the migrated M1/M11/M19 + NEW-2 + NEW-4); --no-run on macOS

cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-control-plane --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/workflow_runtime/mod.rs --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/action_shim/mod.rs --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs kill rate >= 80% (covers the started_digests divergence + the persist/hydrate codec branches)
steps.implementation_notes: This step closes #217 — its ratified scope (input_digest off the start-parameter bytes, NOT the workflow name) is the design grounding; OQ-1 in feature-delta flags that the #217/#40 issue COMMENT threads were not read by the DESIGN subagent (no gh access). Per CLAUDE.md the orchestrator MUST run `gh issue view 217 --comments` and `gh issue view 40 --comments` and confirm D5 matches #217's ratified scope BEFORE this step lands — surface as a blocker if the comments contradict ContentHash::of(&spec.input). NEW-4 (MalformedInput) depends only on the adapter decode path + engine mapping (both landed in 01-03), so it activates cleanly here alongside the #217 work. The schema-evolution fixture is NOT re-touched here — it was wired in 01-02 (see that step's departure note). TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): NEW-2's divergence is a universal property ('for ANY two distinct input byte-vectors of one kind the input_digests differ; for ANY repeated input they match') — frame as proptest over input-byte strategies (Mandate 9). NEW-4 is EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: an engine-boundary sad path enumerated explicitly (one malformed-byte input) per Mandate 11 — compensate with the NEW-2 property on the adjacent digest slot. The migrated M1/M11/M19 retain their existing single-example shape (they verify wiring). Persist INPUTS not derived state (development.md): the persisted intent is the full spec (inputs), input_digest is recomputed from spec.input on demand — never persisted as a derived field.

phases.name: Slice 04 (in scope) — retry-re-drive loop + RetryAttempted journal command + engine-constant budget + engine-minted BudgetExhausted
phases.description: Slice 04. Purely ADDITIVE on the stable Slice-01 body contract (the body return Result<Output, TerminalError> does not change — this is engine growth). The engine classifies transient failures (a ctx.run step Err / a transient WorkflowCtxError), absorbs and re-drives the workflow from the journal up to an engine-constant budget, recomputes attempts from journal RetryAttempted entries against the live policy, and mints TerminalError::budget_exhausted() → WorkflowStatus::Failed { BudgetExhausted } on exhaustion. 04-01 lands the loop + the additive RetryAttempted command + the budget constant + the minting, activating NEW-5/NEW-5b. 04-02 fills the WorkflowBudgetExhaustionMintsTerminal DST invariant (NEW-5's DST sibling) green. Both green because the body contract is unchanged and the journal command is additive (#[serde(default)] per ADR-0063 §2).

steps.name: Engine retry-re-drive loop: RetryAttempted additive journal command, WORKFLOW_RETRY_BUDGET engine constant, transient classification + backoff parking + engine-minted BudgetExhausted; activate NEW-5/NEW-5b
steps.description: Add the retry-re-drive loop to WorkflowEngine, additive on the Slice-01 body contract. (1) Add JournalCommand::RetryAttempted (additive #[serde(default)] command per ADR-0063 §2) recording the attempt INPUT; the sim journal proptest generator (migrated to WorkflowStatus in 01-03) gains the RetryAttempted arm. (2) Add the engine-constant WORKFLOW_RETRY_BUDGET (max attempts + backoff schedule — a function/table analogous to the reconciler RETRY_BACKOFFS, consulted by the engine, NOT persisted per development.md § 'Persist inputs, not derived state'). (3) The engine classifies a transient failure (a ctx.run step Err folded into the step's own T, OR a WorkflowCtxError it deems transient), absorbs it, parks on the backoff window (SimClock-driven), records a RetryAttempted entry, and re-drives from the journal (completed steps replay; the failed step re-fires); attempts are RECOMPUTED from the count of journal RetryAttempted entries + the live policy on each re-drive (the journal is the single durable SSOT — NOT a separate store, contrasting the reconciler RetryMemory View per D4). (4) On budget exhaustion the engine MINTS TerminalError::budget_exhausted() → WorkflowStatus::Failed { BudgetExhausted } (the body authored no failure). (5) ACTIVATE NEW-5 (transient re-driven to budget ⇒ engine-minted BudgetExhausted; body authored nothing) and NEW-5b (transient that clears within budget ⇒ Completed; retryable never reaches the return type) — replace their #[should_panic] bodies.
steps.criteria: JournalCommand::RetryAttempted is an additive command; WORKFLOW_RETRY_BUDGET is an engine constant (not persisted); the engine absorbs/re-drives transient failures from the journal up to the budget with attempts recomputed from journal entries; budget exhaustion mints WorkflowStatus::Failed { BudgetExhausted } the body never authored; NEW-5 and NEW-5b are active and green; the body contract Result<Output, TerminalError> is unchanged from Slice 01.
acceptance_criteria: JournalCommand::RetryAttempted exists as an additive #[serde(default)] command (per ADR-0063 §2) recording the attempt input; an older journal without it decodes cleanly (additive-variant tolerance); the sim journal proptest generator includes a RetryAttempted arm

WORKFLOW_RETRY_BUDGET is an engine-internal constant (max attempts + backoff schedule) consulted by the engine and NOT persisted — `rg 'WORKFLOW_RETRY_BUDGET' crates/` shows it referenced only inside the engine; attempts/last-failure are recomputed from journal RetryAttempted entries against this live policy on each re-drive (per development.md § 'Persist inputs, not derived state')

The engine classifies a transient failure (a ctx.run step Err / a transient WorkflowCtxError), absorbs it (it NEVER reaches the body's Result return type), parks on the backoff window via the injected Clock (SimClock under DST), records a RetryAttempted entry, and re-drives from the journal (completed steps replay byte-equal; the failed step re-fires)

On exhaustion the engine MINTS TerminalError::budget_exhausted() (the engine-only constructor; the author cannot reach it) and projects WorkflowStatus::Failed { terminal } with terminal.kind() == BudgetExhausted

NEW-5 active and green: transient_failures_re_drive_to_budget_then_engine_mints_budget_exhausted — a fixture whose ctx.run step always fails transiently (bumping a shared AtomicUsize) is re-driven exactly WORKFLOW_RETRY_BUDGET times, the terminal row is Failed { BudgetExhausted }, and the journal carries budget-many RetryAttempted commands; the body authored no failure

NEW-5b active and green: transient_failure_that_clears_within_budget_terminates_completed — a fixture whose step fails the first k < budget attempts then succeeds terminates Completed and the step ran k+1 times (the engine re-drove k times, then the success); proves retryable never reaches the return type

The body contract is UNCHANGED from Slice 01 (run(ctx, input) -> Result<Output, TerminalError>); this step adds NO body-side surface — verifiable by no diff to the Workflow trait signature in crates/overdrive-core/src/workflow/mod.rs
cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests passes (incl. NEW-5/NEW-5b); cargo xtask lima run -- cargo dst passes; --no-run on macOS

cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-control-plane --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/workflow_runtime/mod.rs --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/journal/mod.rs kill rate >= 80% (covers the transient-classification + re-drive + exhaustion-minting branches)
steps.implementation_notes: Slice 04 is the one genuinely-deferrable unit per ADR-0065 § Negative consequences — the pre-Slice-04 engine behaviour ('explicit Err(TerminalError) ends the instance') is correct, just less sophisticated. It is IN SCOPE for this roadmap per the dispatch (all priorities, all of Slice 04). PURELY ADDITIVE on the stable body contract: the body return type does not change; this is engine growth + an additive journal command, so the commit is green. OQ-2 (feature-delta): whether attempts are best derived purely from journal entries or need a separate in-memory live-loop counter the journal reconstructs on resume is a Slice-04 design detail that does NOT affect the body contract — resolve during implementation; the journal-derived recompute is the D4 default. The overdrive-core/src/workflow/mod.rs edit in THIS step is ONLY the engine-only TerminalError::budget_exhausted() constructor surface if it was stubbed pub(crate) in 01-01 (it was) — no body-contract change. TEST PARADIGM: NEW-5/NEW-5b are EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM — engine-boundary sad paths enumerated explicitly (a forced-transient fixture re-driven a fixed number of times) per Mandate 11; the determinism rides SimClock. Compensate with the WorkflowBudgetExhaustionMintsTerminal DST property in 04-02 on the adjacent trajectory slot. Per development.md § 'Production code is not shaped by simulation' — the backoff parking uses the injected Clock trait (the same one DST controls); do NOT add a production select! arm whose only purpose is to let SimClock advance.

steps.name: Fill the WorkflowBudgetExhaustionMintsTerminal DST invariant GREEN (NEW-5's DST sibling)
steps.description: Author the WorkflowBudgetExhaustionMintsTerminal DST invariant (ADR-0065 § 'DST invariants', the Slice-04 sibling of WorkflowTerminalStatusProjection and the DST counterpart to NEW-5): under a forced-transient-error workflow, assert the engine re-drives up to the budget and then mints WorkflowStatus::Failed { terminal: BudgetExhausted } — the body never authored a failure (D4). Named enum variant + evaluator in evaluators.rs per the house convention, wired into cargo dst. Additive on 04-01's retry loop — green.
steps.criteria: WorkflowBudgetExhaustionMintsTerminal is a new invariant variant with a real evaluator (no todo!), dispatched by cargo dst; it drives a forced-transient workflow, asserts re-drive-to-budget then engine-minted Failed { BudgetExhausted }, and is seed-reproducible; existing invariants (incl. WorkflowTerminalStatusProjection from 02-01) continue to fire.
acceptance_criteria: WorkflowBudgetExhaustionMintsTerminal exists in overdrive-sim::invariants::Invariant with a fully-implemented evaluator (no todo!/panic); it is wired into the cargo dst dispatch and failure fails the gate

The evaluator drives a workflow whose ctx.run step always fails transiently, advances SimClock past each backoff window so the parked re-drives fire, and asserts the engine re-drove up to WORKFLOW_RETRY_BUDGET then wrote WorkflowStatus::Failed { terminal } with terminal.kind() == BudgetExhausted
The evaluator asserts the body NEVER authored a failure (the transient was a step Err the engine absorbed) — the BudgetExhausted terminal is engine-minted (D4)
Existing invariants (replay_equivalence_provision_record, WorkflowExactlyOnceEffectOnResume, WorkflowTerminalStatusProjection) continue to fire — this variant is additive
The invariant prints its DST seed on failure; cargo dst --seed <N> reproduces bit-identically
cargo xtask lima run -- cargo dst passes with the new invariant active; cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-sim --features integration-tests passes
cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-sim --file crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/evaluators.rs kill rate >= 80%
steps.implementation_notes: The DST counterpart to NEW-5 (the acceptance test in 04-01) — same pattern as 02-01 (an additive DST invariant filled green once the production behaviour it pins exists). Authored GREEN directly (the retry loop landed in 04-01, so every type/behaviour it needs exists — no todo! scaffold phase needed here, unlike the keystone-introduced WorkflowTerminalStatusProjection). TEST PARADIGM: a DST invariant is the paradigm's native assert_eventually!-style structural property over the harness trajectory — no exemption needed. Per .claude/rules/testing.md § 'Properties, not scenarios' the invariant defends the engine-owns-retry / body-owns-only-terminal split (D4) as a forever property, complementing NEW-5's example-based acceptance.
